13.reason: [Using a global variable 'depth' for tracking node height is incorrect because it doesn't reflect the actual depth of each node during traversal; it gets modified globally during recursion, leading to wrong height values for leaf nodes.]

45.reason: [Printing global 'depth' instead of the actual depth of the current node. The global 'depth' does not correctly represent the height of the current leaf node due to shared state across recursive calls.]

48.reason: [The global variable 'depth' is incremented before recursing, but it's shared across all recursive calls. This causes incorrect height tracking because left and right subtrees interfere with each other's depth values. Height should be passed as a parameter instead of using a global variable.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrectly incrementing a global 'depth' before recursing; this affects all recursive paths and leads to inconsistent depth tracking. Depth should be passed as a parameter.]

53.reason: [Same issue: decrementing a global 'depth' after recursion leads to incorrect state when traversing right subtrees after left ones, especially in complex trees. This approach cannot correctly track individual node heights.]
